briefdescription.para: Defines the battery level callback handler function signature.

parameterdescription.para: if the node is connected to an external power source
battery level, where 1 is the minimum and 254 is the maximum value
if the node was not able to measure the battery level

briefdescription.para: Defines the datarate changed callback handler function signature.

parameterdescription.para: Updated datarate.

memberdef.definition: typedef void(* lorawan_link_check_ans_cb_t) (uint8_t demod_margin, uint8_t nb_gateways)
memberdef.name: lorawan_link_check_ans_cb_t

briefdescription.para: Defines the link check answer handler function signature.

parameterdescription.para: The demodulation margin in db.
The number of gateways the device is connected to.

briefdescription.para: Defines the user's descriptor callback handler function signature.

detaileddescription.para: The use of this callback is optional. When Fragmented Data Block Transport is enabled, the application will be notified with the descriptor field present on the FragSessionSetupReq command.

descriptor
Descriptor value given on the FragSessionSetupReq command.
The meaning of Descriptor is application dependent. When doing a FUOTA with a binary image, it may represent the version of the firmware transported.

simplesect.para: 0 if successful. This represents, in the case that the descriptor is the firmware version, that the end-device is able to receive binary firmware. Otherwise, a negative error code (errno.h) indicating the reason for failure. Any negative error code will result in setting the Wrong Descriptor status bit mask when sending FragSessionSetupAns to the Network Server.

briefdescription.para: Register a battery level callback function.

detaileddescription.para: Provide the LoRaWAN stack with a function to be called whenever a battery level needs to be read.
Should no callback be provided the lorawan backend will report 255.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the battery level function

briefdescription.para: Register a callback to be run on downlink packets.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to structure containing callback parameters

briefdescription.para: Register a callback to be called when the datarate changes.

detaileddescription.para: The callback is called once upon successfully joining a network and again each time the datarate changes due to ADR.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to datarate update callback

briefdescription.para: Register a callback to be called when getting answer a to check link request.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to link check ans callback

detaileddescription.para: Join the LoRaWAN network using OTAA or AWB.

parameterdescription.para: Configuration to be used

simplesect.para: 0 if successful, negative errno code if failure

detaileddescription.para: This function need to be called before joining the network.

simplesect.para: 0 if successful, negative errno code if failure

memberdef.argsstring: (uint8_t port, uint8_t *data, uint8_t len, enum lorawan_message_type type)
memberdef.name: lorawan_send

briefdescription.para: Send data to the LoRaWAN network.

detaileddescription.para: Send data to the connected LoRaWAN network.

parameterdescription.para: Port to be used for sending data. Must be set if the payload is not empty.
Data buffer to be sent
Length of the buffer to be sent. Maximum length of this buffer is 255 bytes but the actual payload size varies with region and datarate.
Specifies if the message shall be confirmed or unconfirmed. Must be one of lorawan_message_type.

simplesect.para: 0 if successful, negative errno code if failure

detaileddescription.para: Change the current device class. This function may be called before or after a network connection has been established.

parameterdescription.para: New device class

simplesect.para: 0 if successful, negative errno code if failure

briefdescription.para: Set the number of tries used for transmissions.

parameterdescription.para: Number of tries to be used

simplesect.para: 0 if successful, negative errno code if failure

detaileddescription.para: Control whether adaptive data rate (ADR) is enabled. When ADR is enabled, the data rate is treated as a default data rate that will be used if the ADR algorithm has not established a data rate. ADR should normally only be enabled for devices with stable RF conditions (i.e., devices in a mostly static location).

parameterdescription.para: Enable or Disable adaptive data rate.

detaileddescription.para: Change the default channels mask. When mask is not changed, all the channels can be used for data transmission. Some Network Servers don't use all the channels, in this case, the channels mask must be provided.

parameterdescription.para: Buffer with channels mask to be used.
Size of channels mask buffer.
successful
channels mask or channels mask size is wrong

detaileddescription.para: Change the default data rate.

parameterdescription.para: Data rate used for transmissions

simplesect.para: 0 if successful, negative errno code if failure

detaileddescription.para: The minimum possible datarate may change in response to a TxParamSetupReq command from the network server.
Query the current payload sizes. The maximum payload size varies with datarate, while the current payload size can be less due to MAC layer commands which are inserted into uplink packets.

parameterdescription.para: Maximum payload size for the next transmission
Maximum payload size for this datarate

briefdescription.para: Set the region and frequency to be used.

detaileddescription.para: Control the LoRa region and frequency settings. This should be called before lorawan_start(). If you only have support for a single region selected via Kconfig, this function does not need to be called at all.

parameterdescription.para: The region to be selected

briefdescription.para: Request for time according to DeviceTimeReq MAC cmd.

detaileddescription.para: Append MAC DevTimeReq command. It will be processed on next send message or force sending empty message to request time immediately.

parameterdescription.para: Immediately send an empty message to execute the request

briefdescription.para: Retrieve the current time from LoRaWAN stack updated by DeviceTimeAns on MAC layer.

detaileddescription.para: This function uses the GPS epoch format, as used in all LoRaWAN services.
The GPS epoch started on 1980-01-06T00:00:00Z, but has since diverged from UTC, as it does not consider corrections like leap seconds.

parameterdescription.para: Synchronized time in GPS epoch format truncated to 32-bit.

simplesect.para: 0 if successful, -EAGAIN if the clock is not yet synchronized.

briefdescription.para: Request for link check according to LinkCheckReq MAC cmd.

detaileddescription.para: Append MAC LinkCheckReq command. It will be processed on next send message or force sending empty message to request time immediately.

parameterdescription.para: Immediately send an empty message to execute the request

briefdescription.para: Run Application Layer Clock Synchronization service.

detaileddescription.para: This service sends out its current time in a regular interval (configurable via Kconfig, using CONFIG_LORAWAN_APP_CLOCK_SYNC_PERIODICITY) and receives a correction offset from the application server if the clock deviation is considered too large.
Clock synchronization is required for firmware upgrades over multicast sessions, but can also be used independent of a FUOTA process.

simplesect.para: Available only when the following Kconfig option is enabled: CONFIG_LORAWAN_APP_CLOCK_SYNC.

detaileddescription.para: This function uses the GPS epoch format, as used in all LoRaWAN services.
The GPS epoch started on 1980-01-06T00:00:00Z, but has since diverged from UTC, as it does not consider corrections like leap seconds.

simplesect.para: Available only when the following Kconfig option is enabled: CONFIG_LORAWAN_APP_CLOCK_SYNC.

parameterdescription.para: Synchronized time in GPS epoch format truncated to 32-bit.

simplesect.para: 0 if successful, -EAGAIN if the clock is not yet synchronized.

briefdescription.para: Register a handle descriptor callback function.

detaileddescription.para: Provide to the fragmentation transport service a function to be called whenever a FragSessionSetupReq is received and Descriptor field should be handled.

simplesect.para: Available only when the following Kconfig option is enabled: CONFIG_LORAWAN_FRAG_TRANSPORT.

parameterdescription.para: Callback for notification.

briefdescription.para: Run Fragmented Data Block Transport service.

detaileddescription.para: This service receives fragmented data (usually firmware images) and stores them in the image-1 flash partition.
After all fragments have been received, the provided callback is invoked.

simplesect.para: Available only when the following Kconfig option is enabled: CONFIG_LORAWAN_FRAG_TRANSPORT.

parameterdescription.para: Callback for notification of finished data transfer.

briefdescription.para: Flag to indicate receiving on any port.
